claim.content: Consider electron coupling to marginal-Fermi-liquid fluctuations that renormalize effective mass m^{*}, density of states N^{*}(\theta), and group velocity \mathbf{v}^{*}_{\mathbf{k}}; by analogy with Fermi-liquid transport theory, these renormalizations either cancel exactly in the ratios entering \sigma_{xy} and \kappa_{xy} or else modify only the numerical prefactors of the customary and impurity terms without changing the temperature power laws, so that mass/velocity renormalization does not alter the main conclusion that L_{xy} is a weak function of temperature within a single sample.
